brand.name: Mile Hi City Pickleball
brand.intro: Nine voices. One court. Every week, fresh stories from the players shaping pickleball culture in Colorado.

thisWeek.title: rock music court reveal
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): stand at the entrance to the navy 'GAME ON' court, JOOLA paddle held at chest height, zero expression · let Charli XCX's 'Rock Music' build underneath you. At the stuck-frame glitch around the 8-second mark, the frame freezes on your face, then snaps hard to you touching the GAME ON wall or mid-swing at the baseline. On-screen text over the freeze frame: 'yeah, this is my court.' The glitch is baked into the audio · you just time your cut to it, no special app required, TikTok's built-in edit tools handle it. Film in Creator mode (not Business) since the track is restricted for brand accounts. Post by Wednesday · the XCX wave is moving fast and early posts win the algorithmic slot.
thisWeek.trendSource: Charli XCX posted 'Rock Music' to her own TikTok on June 5, 2026 (@charlixcx/video/7637366549375962390); the audio's baked-in stuck-frame glitch immediately spawned a court-reveal and outfit-reveal format wave across the FYP. NewEngen June 2026 trends report confirms it as the breakout edit format this week. First example URL is a real video from the artist's own account; second is a TikTok discover/search page.

thisWeek.title: first time at mile hi
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): walk in the front door of Mile Hi, phone at chest height filming your own face as you scan all 11 courts · genuinely wide-eyed, say nothing. Use Saxboy Billy's 'The Puerto Rico Song' audio from TikTok and add a text overlay swapping in 'Denver': 'first time in Denver, walked into Mile Hi Pickleball.' No singing required, it just plays under you. Cut to three quick shots: the navy GAME ON wall, picking up a JOOLA paddle off the rack, and a first dink attempt at the kitchen line · miss or make, both work. End on creator smiling at camera, paddle raised: 'told you it was beginner-friendly.' 45-60 seconds, one-take mentality. Tag @milehicitypickleball and pin the location. First impressions only happen once · this is the hardest version of this video and also the most authentic.
thisWeek.trendSource: Saxboy Billy's AI-generated 'The Puerto Rico Song' has 26.2k TikTok posts and 2.5M views on its original upload; Luke Combs, Jennifer Love Hewitt, and the Savannah Bananas all jumped on the 'first time in [place]' discovery format (TMZ + Just Jared, May 2026). That formula maps directly onto a first-visit venue POV. Both example URLs are TikTok discover/search pages · search 'The Puerto Rico Song' in TikTok audio to find the sound.

thisWeek.title: court era unlocked
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): creator in everyday clothes · jeans, oversized tee · holds a JOOLA paddle in front of camera, looks down at their outfit, looks back up with a slight smirk. On-screen text: 'wait, do I need a whole court era now?' Cut to: tenniscore transformation · pleated skirt or bike shorts, polo or cropped crew neck, wristband, court sneakers. Final shot: walking onto the navy 'GAME ON' court at Mile Hi, paddle at side, full court-era energy. Voiceover optional: 'Coach Jill said dress for the game you want. I listened.' 30-45 seconds. Film the 'before' at home, the 'after' arrival at Mile Hi Court 1 · the contrast between your bedroom and the retro navy court does all the heavy lifting. End with comment bait: 'what does your court era look like?' No specific audio needed · use whatever is trending on your FYP that week.
thisWeek.trendSource: Tenniscore has 70M+ TikTok views on #tennisskirts and is accelerating into summer 2026 as Gen Z's racquet-sport aesthetic · pleated skirts, cropped polos, and wristbands worn off-court are now mainstream (Heuritech tenniscore trend analysis + Hibbett off-court tennis fits report, 2026). Pickleball is close enough that any tenniscore creator stepping onto a Mile Hi court is a natural crossover moment. The #tennisskirts URL is a real TikTok tag page; the second URL is a discover/search page.

thisWeek.title: sad girl found the kitchen
thisWeek.brief: Olivia Rodrigo's album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' drops June 12 · the FYP is about to flood with emotional testimonials. Hook (0-3s): stare straight into camera, no smile, on-screen text: 'you seem pretty sad for a girl who just found pickleball.' One beat pause. Cut to: three real-reactions · a fist pump after a winning dink, a laugh after a terrible shot, a high-five with a stranger on the Mile Hi courts. If you're a genuine beginner, lean all the way in: 'I cried a tiny bit during my first drill. Coach Jill didn't notice. I came back the next day.' End frame on the GAME ON wall. 30-45 seconds. Film this June 12-13 when the album audio is top of FYP and competition for the format is lowest. Real emotion beats polished every single time for this format · one take, no filters.
thisWeek.trendSource: Olivia Rodrigo's third album 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' drops June 12, 2026 · lyric-overlay testimonial and 'sad era to glow-up' narrative content is forecast to dominate the FYP June 11-16 (NewEngen June 2026 trends report). Social-proof testimonials using the album title as the setup line are already appearing in pre-drop content. Both example URLs are TikTok discover/search pages · use the album title as the audio search term on TikTok on or after June 12.

creators.name: Quinn Gordon
creators.vibe: 2 videos at root · IG/TikTok pending confirm

belleComments.intro: Good job, squad! Videos are looking strong this week. Real love for the music sync on the edits, the storytelling about coming into Mile Hi (showing the doors, the desk, the courts), and the focus on what the venue actually feels like. That's the squad voice landing. A few small brand things to lock in across all 9 of you so the grid feels like ONE roster instead of nine different accounts. These aren't call-outs · just patterns I noticed this week that we can all level up on.

rules.title: captions · same look every time
rules.body: When everyone uses a different font, the feed looks off. Let's all use white text with a black outline so the squad reads as one team. The full recipe lives in section 07 'before you post' at the bottom of this portal. Peek at it before you edit.

rules.title: pov format · keep it the same
rules.body: Always go with 'mile hi : POV' · logo, then colon, then POV in all caps. Same format every video. Makes us instantly recognizable when someone scrolls past.

rules.title: caption position · center it
rules.body: Drop your caption right in the middle. If you can't center, nudge it just above the middle. The bottom of the screen is where the TikTok UI sits, so text down there gets covered up and nobody reads it.

rules.title: always have a caption
rules.body: Every video gets text on screen. Yes, even the dance ones (especially the dance ones). The caption is what makes you searchable AND what holds viewers who aren't already into pickleball.

rules.title: use the mile hi joola paddle
rules.body: Grab a Mile Hi JOOLA paddle before you film. Your own paddle, a Monarch, a friend's, those don't read as Mile Hi when someone scrolls past. The brand paddle IS the brand signal.

rules.title: keep it in color
rules.body: Mile Hi is bright. The court is orange and navy, the vibe is energy. Please skip black-and-white filters and moody desaturation. Color always.

rules.title: music · keep the energy up
rules.body: Mile Hi is energy + community, so default to songs that lift the room. Exception · if a trend card at the top of the portal specifically calls for a slower or moody song (it'll happen sometimes), that's fine, follow the trend. Otherwise, if a song makes YOU feel sluggish, it'll make your viewer scroll.

rules.title: follow the trend cards at the top
rules.body: Sometimes a trend will feel a little weird and that's okay. We pick them on purpose · they're part of our strategy and the data shows what's going to work this week. Trust the cards even when they don't make sense in the moment.

belleComments.outro: For everyone just starting · you're doing GREAT. These are small tweaks, not rewrites. Pick the 2 easiest for you and lock them in this week. The squad that iterates wins.

images.label: image 1
images.caption: Wrong paddle (Monarch). Use the Mile Hi JOOLA paddle. The brand paddle IS the signal on the FYP.

images.label: image 2
images.caption: Caption parked at the bottom. Pull it to center or slightly above. Nobody reads the lower third.

images.label: image 3
images.caption: Caption blends into the background. Switch to white text with a black outline so it pops on any shot.

images.label: image 4
images.caption: Story shot with no caption. Every clip needs text on screen, even the establishing ones. Tell the viewer what they're looking at in 2 seconds.

images.label: image 5
images.caption: Dance shot with no paddles in hand AND the logo on the wall is mirrored (filmed front-facing without flipping back). Keep paddles in the frame on dance trends, and un-mirror your front-camera clips.

images.label: image 6
images.caption: POV format is off. Use 'mile hi : POV' (logo, then colon, then POV in all caps). Lock this format every time.

images.label: image 7
images.caption: Black-and-white / desaturated filter. Mile Hi is color + court + energy. No grayscale, no moody.

images.label: image 8
images.caption: POV without the colon + the logo prefix. Same fix as image 6 · 'mile hi : POV' is the format.

images.label: image 9
images.caption: Caption font doesn't match the squad recipe. Quicksand Bold lowercase + Anton caps for the punch word. Same across everyone, every video.

images.label: image 10
images.caption: Caption at the bottom AND overlapping the orange court line. Hard to read. Center the text or push it slightly above middle.

belleComments.comment: Organize your Drive folder, every time. RAW = everything straight off your phone (even the takes you don't love). EDITED = your final cut, ready to post. We pull from EDITED only · if it's not there, it doesn't get posted. Takes 30 seconds to drag, saves everyone hours.

When you film the dance or lip-sync trends, ALWAYS keep the paddle in your hands. Even mid-spin, mid-twirl, mid-beat-drop. The paddle is the brand signal that makes us instantly recognizable on the FYP. Without it, the video could be from any sport, so please keep it on you the whole time.

weeklyReport.summary: Production is rolling, 16 edited clips already in Drive this week. Top of the squad at 4 edits, median jumped to 2. Four trends live (Charli XCX glitch reveal, first-visit POV, tenniscore transformation, Olivia Rodrigo album moment). Live TikTok numbers come once the posts start hitting feeds.

topCreator.name: The squad
topCreator.reason: 16 edits this week, biggest production push yet. Two creators tied at 4, three at 2 or 3, real momentum across the roster.

watchOuts: Two creators still don't have a Drive folder set up. If that's you, ping Tricia so we can get yours created this week.
A few creators are sitting on raws with no edits yet. If that's you, drop one edit before Sunday and you're locked in for the week.
Reminder: name your post-ready folder 'Edited' (not 'Completed Vidoes' or root level) so the auto-counter picks it up cleanly.
If your IG handle on the sheet doesn't match the account you actually use, send Belle the right one. The leaderboard pulls from there.
weeklyReport.production: 16 edited videos across the squad this week. Top of production at 4, median at 2.
